The case involves FIRs under the Bharatiya Nyay Sanhita and Dowry Prohibition Act.

This can lead to uncontrolled blood sugar, weight gain, and long-term complications like diabetic retinopathy. Experts advise incorporating movement breaks, limiting screen time before bed, and mindful technology use to manage these risks.

description: Sai Sudharsan and Shubman Gill etched their names in IPL history with remarkable performances against Chennai Super Kings. Both batters surpassed 600 runs for the second consecutive season, a feat achieved by only a select few. Their dominant opening partnership also saw them climb various T20 and IPL record lists.

42 MLAs skip oath in God’s name during Kerala assembly swearing-in ceremony
description: Kerala's 16th Legislative Assembly's swearing-in ceremony showcased diversity as MLAs chose different languages and opted for solemn affirmations or oaths in God's name. Political rivals displayed camaraderie, and CM V.D. Satheesan reiterated his full name, addressing recent caste-related criticism. A symbolic gesture of reconciliation occurred between Pro-tem Speaker G. Sudhakaran and Pinarayi Vijayan.

description: Cricket legend Dilip Vengsarkar is absolutely fuming! He's slammed the selectors for completely snubbing Auqib Nabi, the Ranji Trophy's top bowler, for the Afghanistan Test.  Despite Nabi's incredible 60 wickets and being named Player of the Tournament for leading J&K to their first title, they apparently passed him over due to 'pace concerns'.

title: IPL: Ruturaj Gaikwad penalised after CSK’s crushing defeat to Gujarat Titans
description: Chennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad has been fined INR 24 lakh for a slow over-rate, marking their second offense this IPL 2026 season. The penalty came as Gujarat Titans crushed CSK by 89 runs, eliminating them from playoff contention. GT's strong batting, led by Gill and Sudharsan, set a massive target, while CSK's chase faltered significantly.

Spying for Pakistan: Punjab man held for using highway CCTV to track Army movement
description: A Pathankot resident, Baljit Singh, has been arrested for allegedly spying for Pakistan-based handlers. Singh confessed to receiving directions from Dubai and being paid Rs 40,000.

Autopsy revealed severe injuries, and viscera samples are undergoing forensic analysis.

Microsoft AI CEO gave these white-collar jobs 12-18 months before they are fully automated
description: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman predicts white-collar jobs like lawyers and accountants could be fully automated within 12-18 months. He emphasizes AI will transform roles, shifting humans to oversight and strategic tasks, similar to how software engineers now use AI for coding.  Doctors, for instance, may focus more on patient care and emotional support as AI handles diagnoses.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
description: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.
